message.content: Yes. Dropping from 5.2% to 4.1% on a 30-year fixed typically cuts your monthly payment enough that you’d recoup $6,000 in closing costs in well under 7 years—often in roughly 2–4 years, depending on your loan balance and how much time is left. After the breakeven point, the lower rate is pure savings during the rest of your 7+ year stay. Just make sure you’re not resetting far into a new 30-year term without considering the extra total interest; if needed, ask the lender for a shorter term (e.g., 25 or 20 years) at the new rate.
